35.reason: Operator precedence error: `c = (fgetc(in) != EOF)` assigns 0 or 1 to c, not the character. Should be `(c = fgetc(in)) != EOF`.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Operator precedence error: `c = (fgetc(in) != EOF)` assigns 0 or 1 to c, not the character. Should be `(c = fgetc(in)) != EOF`.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Operator precedence error: `c=fgetc(in)!=EOF` is parsed as `c = (fgetc(in) != EOF)`, so `c` gets 1 or 0 instead of the character. Should be `(c = fgetc(in)) != EOF`.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Operator precedence: this assigns c = (fgetc(in)!=EOF), so c is always 0 or 1, not the actual character. Should be while((c=fgetc(in))!=EOF)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Assignment and comparison operator precedence error: should be (c=fgetc(in))!=EOF, otherwise c is always 0 or 1, never the actual character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The original code `c=fgetc(in)!=EOF` assigns the boolean result of the comparison (0 or 1) to c, due to operator precedence. It should be `(c=fgetc(in))!=EOF`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Operator precedence error: != has higher precedence than =, so c was assigned the boolean result (0 or 1) instead of the character. Added parentheses to fix.]

60.reason: Missing initialization: sheet[k].count should be set to 1 for the newly added word.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing initialization: sheet[k].count should be set to 1 for the newly added word.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing count initialization: after copying the word, sheet[k].count should be set to 1; otherwise it holds garbage value.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Moved strcpy before incrementing num or just use k. The logic `else if(k==num)` implies k is the new index.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Initialization error: The count of the new word must be initialized to 1. Previously it contained garbage value.]
